title: December 2025 Clean and Safe Team Homeless Actions and Outcomes
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 11, 2025
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement & Services
SUBJECT: December 2025 Clean & Safe Team Homeless Actions & Outcomes

The City of Reno continues to address homeless-related concerns and provide outreach to the unsheltered community within city limits through the Clean and Safe Team. The Clean and Safe Team plays a critical role in addressing the multifaceted challenges and community impacts associated with homelessness, while coordinating multiple concurrent efforts and programs to ensure a comprehensive, citywide response.

Clean and Safe Team Departments
• Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Community Engagement and Services – Clean and Safe, Reno Direct
• City Manager's Office - Security
• Housing and Neighborhood Development
• Maintenance and Operations
• Parks and Recreation
• Reno Police – Community Action and Outreach (CAO), Mobile Engagement Team (MET), Parking Enforcement

The following is an overview of December 2025 community reported concerns and Clean and Safe Team outputs for outreach and interactions, enforcement and compliance, and maintenance and restoration.

December 2025 Key Highlights
• 69 Outreach Interactions
• 9 Individuals into Transitional Housing
• 201 Citations
• 127 Arrests
• 3 Large Encampment Clean-Ups
• 27 Smaller Encampment Clean-Ups
• 182 Yards of Trash Collected
• 42 Occupied Vehicles Posted
• 5 Occupied Vehicles Towed
• 4 Park Exclusions
• 40 Code Enforcement Cases
• 174 Allied Security Site Visits

Reno Direct Community Service Request Overview
December 2025

Service Request Type | Number of Requests | Percentage
Homeless Concerns | 79 | 29.26%
Trespassing on Private Property | 46 | 17.04%
Camping in City Parks | 8 | 2.96%
Camping on Truckee River | 8 | 2.96%
Occupied Vehicle Nuisance | 129 | 47.78%
Total | 270 | 100%

Month-over-Month Comparison

Service Request Type | November | December | Percentage Change
Homeless Concerns | 95 | 79 | 17% Decrease
Trespassing on Private Property | 57 | 46 | 19% Decrease
Camping in City Parks | 14 | 8 | 43% Decrease
Camping on Truckee River | 3 | 8 | 167% Increase
Occupied Vehicle Nuisance | 127 | 129 | 2% Increase
Total | 296 | 270 | 9% Decrease

Outreach & Interactions

Clean and Safe Interactions | Housing Arrangements | Shelter Arrangements | Identification Replacements | Resources Accessed
69 | 9 | 2 | 1 | 26

Outreach Interactions

Location | # Interactions | # Accepted | Outcomes
West 4th Street/Reno Rock | 1 | 0 | Arrived on site with Park Rangers to assess the area after receiving an escalated service request. Spoke with an individual about housing opportunities, as the individual's partner receives SSDI.
8th Street Park | 1 | 1 | Met with an individual who just left a 30-day program, who was interested in Safe Camp and services. Transported the individual to the Resource Center to access the overflow shelter.
Severn Drive | 2 | 0 | Met with client to fill out Safe Camp paperwork.
Sage Street & train tracks | 6 | 0 | Informed individuals on-site about the upcoming cleanup. The individuals declined services.
Believe Plaza | 8 | 5 | Made an appointment with a family of 4 to fill out motel assistance paperwork. Made an appointment with a family of 5 to fill out motel assistance paperwork and Neighborly application.
Motel 6 | 4 | 4 | Assisted family of 4 with moving and transport to a motel.
Resource Center | 1 | 1 | Facilitated move-in to Safe Camp.
City Hall | 5 | 5 | Assisted family of 5 in filling out motel assistance paperwork.
Safe Street & train tracks | 8 | 1 | Transported an individual to the Resource Center for an intake assessment for the Permanent Supportive Housing program.
Resource Center | 2 | 2 | Met with a couple to fill out a housing application. Informed the couple the couple were approved for rental and deposit assistance.
Cheryl Apartments | 3 | 2 | Transported couple to Cheryl Apartments for scheduled move-in. Found out that the unit was on the 2nd floor and the couple could not go up and down the stairs. Diverted the couple into a motel room through the motel assistance program.
Siegel Suites – Nevadan | 5 | 5 | Arrived on site with the check from RHA to pay for the family's motel expense.
Motel 6 | 2 | 2 | Met with a couple to fill out motel assistance paperwork. The couple could not find the couple's award letter and the couple will let Kaytlin Canner know once the couple finds the award letter.
Wells Footbridge | 2 | 2 | Met with 2 individuals to fill out paperwork for the Permanent Supportive Housing program as the 2 individuals are eligible. Both individuals need an ID and Social Security card.
Motel 6 | 2 | 2 | Followed up with a couple to fill out paperwork for the RHA HPP program. The couple could not find updated award letter.
4th Street & Sage Street | 1 | 1 | Spoke with one individual and discussed mental health treatment and getting the individual set up with Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) and case management at Safe Camp.
Resource Center | 1 | 1 | Met with client to fill out Safe Camp paperwork.
Dickerson/W 4th Street | 1 | 1 | Arrived on site for cleanup operation along the W. 4th Street/Stoker area. Nobody was interested in services.
Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) | 2 | 1 | Arrived on site to get a replacement ID for 2 individuals. One of the 2 individuals never had an ID in Nevada, so the individual could not get a replacement.
Motel 6 on Wells | 2 | 2 | Extended a motel stay for two clients and received an updated photo of the client's award letter.
Resource Center | 1 | 1 | Facilitated a Safe Camp move-in.
J Resort | 1 | 1 | Assisted a client with moving from the J Resort to the Siegel Suites, while the client waits for a housing unit through Reno Housing Authority.
Hampton House | 1 | 1 | Met with a client who was on 30-day sit out from Cares Campus. Assisted the client with filling out an appeal form.
Total | 69 | 26 |

Enforcement and Compliance

Reno Police Department
Reno Police Department's Community Action and Outreach (CAO) and Mobile Engagement Team (MET) outcomes:
• Unsheltered Contacts: 177
• Posted Occupied Vehicles: 42
• Vehicles Towed: 5
• Citations: 187
• Felony Arrests: 31
• Misdemeanor Arrests: 96

Park and River Rangers
In December, the Park and River Rangers conducted 605 site-visits. The Park and River Rangers made 111 interactions with unsheltered individuals, which included issuing warnings, citations, or providing service referrals.

Park and River Rangers enforcement efforts in City Parks and along the Truckee River:
• Citations issued: 14
o 14 citations issued for sitting, lying, or camping on a public place
• Park exclusions issued: 4
o 2 Stewart Park
o 1 Brodhead Park
o 1 Fisherman's Park #2

Code Enforcement
Code Enforcement cases related to homeless related concerns on private property:
• Code Enforcement Cases: 40
• Courtesy Letters: 21
• Warning of Violation: 10
• First Administrative Citation: 0
• Second Administrative Citation: 1
• Third Administrative Citation: 0

Allied Universal Security
There are two Allied Universal Security officers dedicated to

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.tldr: The Clean and Safe Team's December 2025 report summarizes homeless outreach, enforcement, and maintenance activities across Reno. Key outcomes include 69 outreach interactions, 9 individuals placed into transitional housing, 127 arrests, and 30 encampment clean-ups. Community service requests totaled 270, a 9% decrease from November, with occupied vehicle nuisance comprising nearly half of all requests. Multiple city departments and contracted security coordinated efforts citywide.
fields.key_stats: 69 outreach interactions; 9 individuals into transitional housing; 2 shelter arrangements; 1 ID replacement; 26 resources accessed; 201 citations (187 RPD + 14 Park/River Rangers); 127 arrests (31 felony, 96 misdemeanor); 3 large encampment clean-ups; 27 smaller encampment clean-ups; 182 yards of trash collected; 42 occupied vehicles posted; 5 occupied vehicles towed; 4 park exclusions; 40 code enforcement cases; 174 Allied Security site visits; 605 Park and River Ranger site visits; 270 total Reno Direct service requests (9% decrease from November)
fields.action_items: Couple at Motel 6 to provide updated award letter to Kaytlin Canner; two individuals at Wells Footbridge need ID and Social Security card obtained for Permanent Supportive Housing program eligibility; couple diverted to motel assistance program after Cheryl Apartments unit was inaccessible due to stairs

Reno Police Department – Mobile Engagement Team (MET)
